sections.paragraph:
αἰνέω fut. αἰνέσω, 1 aor. ᾔνεσα LXX to praise (so Hom. et al.; LXX; TestSol 12:5; TestAbr, Just. The primary idea: ‘express approval’ cp. IG IX/1, 119, 8.) in our lit. used only of the praise of God (Diog. L. 1, 39; very oft. LXX; TestAbr B 14, p. 119, 2 [Stone p. 86]; Just., A I, 13, 1, D. 106, 1; PGM 4, 1146) αἰ. τὸν θεόν Lk 2:13, 20; 19:37; 24:53 D; Ac 2:47; 3:8f; cp. MPol 14:3. τὸν κύριον Ro 15:11 (Ps 116:1). τῷ θεῷ ἡμῶν Rv 19:5 (the dat. corresp. to לְ w. הוֹדָה and הִלֵּל LXX e.g. Jer 20:13; 1 Ch 16:36; 2 Ch 5:13; B-D-F §187, 4); cp. B 7:1. W. εὐλογεῖν (Da 5:23) Lk 24:53 v.l. (s. GKilpatrick in Essays in Memory of GHCMacgregor ’65, 19). Of praise of gods by polytheists Dg 2:7 (cp. Judg 16:24 A; Da 5:23). Cp. αἶνος.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

dat. dat. = dative

corresp. corresp. = corresponding(ly)

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 27
